page: Microsoft Azure F4-1ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 server family. It features 1 dedicated vCPU powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance type does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the hardware is premium storage capable. There are zero integrated GPUs, and the configuration does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high memory density relative to the single dedicated vCPU, this server type offers a specialized qualitative cost profile. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel CPU processing power. Suitable workloads include small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, lightweight data buffering, and memory-bound utility applications.
description: Microsoft Azure F4-1ams_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine within the Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The hardware configuration features 1 dedicated vCPU powered by an AMD processor, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This establishes an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. The server does not include any local storage, meaning it relies entirely on external premium storage options, and it features no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This memory-intensive profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring significant memory capacity without the need for multi-core CPU scaling. Appropriate workloads include small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, data buffering, and memory-bound utility applications.
